evals.prompt: This dashboard page is already fully functional and generally follows the intended layout. There is no major design drift, but it still feels flat and unfinished. Run a fidelity check first, then polish only if alignment is already acceptable. Do not change routing, data flow, or API usage.
evals.expected_output: Performs a fidelity-first check, concludes drift is low, then chooses polish mode while keeping product behavior unchanged.

evals.prompt: The page works, but it no longer matches our ui-design tokens, component spec, and latest screenshots under .allforai/ui-design/screenshots. Run a fidelity check and restore the implementation so it is materially closer to the design contract without rewriting business logic.
evals.expected_output: Chooses restore mode after identifying meaningful design drift, uses available design artifacts, and prioritizes fidelity recovery over freeform aesthetic invention.

evals.prompt: Do not build a new feature. Review this already-implemented settings screen and decide whether the work needed is restore or polish. Explain the fidelity check result first, then refine the UI like a senior frontend engineer would: clearer primary action, cleaner visual grouping, better empty/loading/error states, same behavior.
evals.expected_output: Behaves like a fidelity-first post-implementation UI refinement skill, reports the drift assessment clearly, and respects the existing feature boundary.

evals.prompt: This admin page looks nicer after a recent refactor, but several styles are now hardcoded and no longer follow tokens.json. The layout is also denser than the approved screenshot. Use a restore-before-polish workflow and avoid introducing any new feature work.
evals.expected_output: Identifies token drift and density drift as fidelity issues, restores the baseline first, and only adds polish if the screen is aligned afterward.
